title: LaneLoom — Branded Top-Down Traffic Shorts: Reusable Asset Kit, Color-Graded Frames, Vertical Cover + VO/Reel Prep
one_line_ask: Build the LaneLoom branded source-asset kit for the top-down traffic shorts: vectorize reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG, apply one consistent color grade across the reference frames, reframe the graded junction frame to a 9:16 vertical cover/end-card and produce a stock-backed road texture, then clean the scratch VO and reframe the rough preview clip to vertical — all chained so the deliverable kit emerges from the supplied plates.

qc.transcript: At a four-way stop, the first vehicle to arrive has the right of way. This is crucial for maintaining order. If two cars stop at the same time, the driver on the right goes first. Always make eye contact, signal your intent, and roll forward only when the intersection is clear. Remember, clear commu

issues: Cars are all depicted as sedans/similar body styles rather than clearly differentiated sedan, SUV, and compact variants
Yellow diamond sign is blank with no text/symbol, which is acceptable as a hazard placeholder but slightly ambiguous
Minor: turn-arrow for 'straight' is a simple upward arrow rather than a bold rounded shape consistent with the other arrow styles
Slight luminance noise visible in the transparent/checkerboard region (acceptable for source file)
Location pin icon is slightly small relative to wordmark height but within acceptable range
Slight photorealistic texture on asphalt may read as photograph rather than pure vector-illustration style, though acceptable as a graphic reference frame
Lane markings on left and right roads show slight asymmetry in arrow placement
Dark hatchback (bottom) is positioned slightly right of center lane, partially overlapping the right-turn lane
Crosswalk stripe count is inconsistent across the four crossings (minor)
Image is 3:4 portrait crop rather than 1024x1024 square as specified in brief

assets.name: ae_preview_lanechange_16x9.mp4

issues: Car is slightly rotated/angled which adds minor pseudo-3D feel, but acceptable for motion keyframe
Shadow under car adds subtle depth inconsistent with pure flat 2D style

assets.note: Migrated to Seedance 2.0 (t2v) on 2026-07-09 for higher quality. Flat 2D top-down driving-education explainer clip: single generic teal car mid lane-change on a multi-lane dashed road. 16:9 1920x1080 so the downstream vertical reframe is a real task. Silent raw source footage — the task audio comes from the separate VO track. Brand-clean (abstract vector car glyph, no logos/badges/trademarks) and scene-correct flat-2D (not photoreal/3D) on 2.0, so no 1.0 fallback needed.
